task_prompt_summary: New developer asked for go/no-go on skipping exploration in unfamiliar repo; requested minimum pre-implementation checklist

process_track_selection.note: Correctly selected Standard track for medium-sized feature in unfamiliar codebase

go_no_go_recommendation.note: Clear NO-GO recommendation with direct citation of skill principle 'Understand before acting'

checklist_completeness.note: Checklist covers all required pre-implementation phases (Discovery, Exploration, Questions, Design). Slightly docked because TDD and code-reviewer/code-simplifier phases are mentioned only as context, not surfaced as pre-implementation requirements (they are post-implementation, which is correct — no deduction warranted on reflection)

checklist_actionability.note: Concrete agent prompts provided, green-light conditions specified, checkboxes are executable. Minor gap: no explicit 'read these files' naming since no codebase is available.

skill_fidelity.note: Response follows the Default Response Shape (Understanding, Relevant Findings, Recommendation, Open Questions). Correctly avoids artificial approval gates while still blocking premature implementation. Correctly identifies exploration as non-optional on Standard track.

response_shape_compliance.note: Uses Understanding, Relevant Findings, Recommendation, Open Questions sections. Missing 'Implementation Map' and 'Artifact Status' — appropriate omissions since no implementation is being done yet, but could have noted handoff artifact would be created at design phase.

conciseness_vs_completeness.note: Response is thorough and actionable. Slightly longer than necessary — the closing note on 'moving quickly' is helpful context but adds length.

open_questions_quality.note: One high-leverage question asked (what is the feature). Correct to not manufacture more questions. Could have noted that the feature name would also determine whether Deep track is more appropriate.

key_observations: Correctly applied the skill's core principle: exploration is mandatory, not optional, especially for new developers in unfamiliar repos.
Provided concrete, executable agent prompts for the exploration phase rather than vague guidance.
Did not manufacture artificial approval gates beyond what the pre-implementation checklist requires.
Green-light conditions section is a strong addition not explicitly in the skill but consistent with its spirit.
No handoff artifact created, which is appropriate since no implementation occurred and the task was advisory in nature.
The 'moving quickly' framing directly addresses the developer's stated concern and the lead's suggestion — good contextual awareness.
failure_modes_not_triggered: Did not skip exploration phase endorsement under time pressure
Did not give a go recommendation to please the user/lead
Did not manufacture excessive questions before giving the recommendation
Did not jump to implementation planning before resolving the go/no-go question
